title: CI/CD deploy pipeline (GHCR -> homelab, wc-predict.davewil.dev)
description: Replicate the slackex deploy pattern for predictex: GitHub Actions builds a Phoenix release Docker image, pushes to GHCR, SSHes to the homelab Docker host (192.168.1.102 via Tailscale), pulls/migrates/recreates. caddy-docker-proxy serves wc-predict.davewil.dev with TLS via Cloudflare DNS challenge. Postgres on default network only; app joins default + proxy.
notes: Pipeline built, committed, and locally verified (docker build OK, 88 tests, compose valid). All 6 GitHub secrets set (SECRET_KEY_BASE + POSTGRES_PASSWORD generated; DEPLOY_HOST/DEPLOY_SSH_KEY/TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID/TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY copied from Vaultwarden). Remaining: push a v* tag to run the first real deploy end-to-end (Tailscale SSH -> migrate -> caddy TLS for wc-predict.davewil.dev). Close after first green deploy.
close_reason: CI/CD pipeline shipped and proven — deployed v0.2.x, v0.3.0, v0.4.0, v0.4.1 cleanly (build -> GHCR -> Tailscale SSH -> homelab migrate/recreate -> /health). Stale in_progress.

title: LiveView: Admin (results sync, cohort %, players, recompute)
description: Trigger Results.Ingest sync, manual result override, per-fixture FIFA cohort % entry (drives the risky bonus), player management, and recompute scores.
notes: Includes admin manual entry of predictions ON BEHALF OF players (from submitted FIFA screenshots) — the guaranteed-path fallback when auto-import (xox) is unavailable. My Predictions (79q, done) only displays these; entry lives here.
close_reason: Admin console shipped in v0.4.0: prediction entry on behalf of players (by-player + by-fixture audit), fixtures sync/override/cohort, player promote. Reviewed + booster-on-blank fix.

title: LiveView: Leaderboard + WhatsApp export
description: Overall + per-round standings sourced from the DB-backed leaderboard (predictex-0ae), with a 'Copy WhatsApp text' button for sharing in the league chat.

title: Player authentication (phx.gen.auth)
description: Run phx.gen.auth for the Player schema and reconcile with the existing minimal players table (display_name, is_admin already present). Provides login for the ~15 league members.

title: LiveView: My Predictions
description: Read-only personal dashboard at /predictions: a member's imported FIFA picks, per-fixture scoring, booster marker, kickoff lock state, 'no pick imported' warning, rank/total reconciled with the leaderboard, World Cup theme (flags), round tabs, and an outbound FIFA link. NO prediction entry here (admin entry = a02, auto-import = xox).
close_reason: Read-only My Predictions dashboard implemented, reviewed, 216 tests green, runtime + visual smoke confirmed.

title: DB-backed leaderboard
description: Add a Predictex.Standings context that scores DB-backed predictions (players, predictions, fixtures) by reusing the pure Predictex.Scoring engine, and a leaderboard query. Mirror the JSON-based Leaderboard.build/3 logic but source data from Ecto. The existing mix predictex.leaderboard stays as the no-DB tool.

title: Implement Predictex.dc.html design (theme + screens)
description: Apply the claude.ai/design handoff bundle (Predictex.dc.html) to the live app: daisyUI pitch-green+gold theme, Archivo/Spline fonts, FixtureCard+LeaderboardRow atoms, Leaderboard champion-spotlight, My Predictions, Auth, Admin visual language, flourish keyframes. Dark default. Preserve all phx- bindings/ids/hooks.
close_reason: Implemented Predictex.dc.html design: themed app.css (pitch-green/gold, dark default) + fonts, fixture_card/leaderboard_row/auth_card atoms, champion-spotlight leaderboard, branded My Predictions/auth/navbar/admin. 275 tests pass; verified in browser. Deferred items filed as follow-ups.

title: Auto-sync FIFA cohort % (risky-bonus data) from matchStats.json
description: Spike (docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-16-xox-fifa-import-spike.md) found FIFA publishes per-outcome cohort percentages at play.fifa.com/json/match_predictor/matchStats.json, keyed by matchId, server-fetchable (HTTP 200 plain curl, NOT Akamai-gated). homeWin/draw/awayWin map exactly to cohort_home_pct/cohort_draw_pct/cohort_away_pct. Today these are admin-entered (a02) and the risky bonus is silently skipped when unset. Build a plain Oban worker (mt6 substrate): Req.get matchStats.json + rounds.json, map matchId -> Fixture (crosswalk by kickoff date/teams), upsert cohort_*_pct. No bookmarklet, no member action. Also exposes firstGoalIsOwn + results as a cross-check. Separate from xox (public stats, not member predictions).
close_reason: Shipped in v0.5.0. mt6: hourly... ResultSync Oban worker (every 15m) runs Ingest.sync_from_url. 7ux: CohortSync Oban worker (hourly) pulls FIFA matchStats.json, pure Fifa.Cohort.plan joins by {utc_date,team-set} + orientation, overwrites cohort_*_pct.

title: Automated result-sync schedule
description: Run Results.Ingest on a schedule (Oban or a Task) so fixtures/results refresh automatically during the tournament. Verify the source ft excludes ET before trusting it for knockout scoring.
close_reason: Shipped in v0.5.0. mt6: hourly... ResultSync Oban worker (every 15m) runs Ingest.sync_from_url. 7ux: CohortSync Oban worker (hourly) pulls FIFA matchStats.json, pure Fifa.Cohort.plan joins by {utc_date,team-set} + orientation, overwrites cohort_*_pct.

title: FIFA prediction import (bookmarklet + /api/import)
description: Auth-safe import: bookmarklet runs in the player's own play.fifa.com session, POSTs only prediction JSON to /api/import authenticated with our session. Capture the FIFA endpoint via DevTools (site 403s scripted requests). Paste-JSON and manual entry fallbacks.
notes: Group-stage scoreline+booster self-import CODE COMPLETE & REVIEWED (5 tasks, subagent-driven, two-stage review each + final integration review: Ready). Modules: Fifa.Crosswalk (shared matching), Fifa.Reference (shared FIFA fetch), Fifa.Import (pure plan/3, composite {round,matchId} key), ImportLive (/import preview/confirm + paste fallback + bookmarklet + colocated FifaFragment hook). 275 tests green. Commits 1098f4a..097a09b (local, unpushed). REMAINING GATE: manual real-session bookmarklet validation (operator's own FIFA login -> /import preview -> confirm) per spec acceptance criterion; not yet done. Deferred: knockout import (new issue), derive @import_url from endpoint (new issue).

title: Highlight current player ('YOU') on the public leaderboard
description: leaderboard_row/1 already supports a 'you' attr (green highlight + YOU badge). Not wired on the public '/' route because LeaderboardLive has no current_scope (route is in a bare scope, not a live_session with mount_current_scope). Add mount_current_scope on_mount to the '/' route and pass you={s.player_id == current_scope.player.id} when logged in.

title: Event-triggered match-day flourishes (rank climb / exact / booster toast on results sync)
description: Design section 07 + footer item ②: celebratory CSS toasts on match-day events. The keyframes (.animate-pdx-rise/-pop/-glow) and .font-score ship in app.css and a static rise is applied to the champion hero. The triggering is backend work: detect rank-climb / exact / booster-hit on results sync and broadcast (PubSub) a toast to the player. Wire as a daisyUI toast + animate- utility.

title: Per-fixture points breakdown + risky-pick banner on FixtureCard
description: Design (Predictex.dc.html, footer item ③) shows per-fixture scoring breakdown chips (Outcome +10 / Home +5 / Away +5 / GD +5 / Exact +5) and a 'Risky pick paid off — only N% backed it' banner. Deferred from the design implementation because Dashboard exposes only total fixture points (not the component breakdown) and no risky flag. Needs Predictex.Scoring to expose a per-prediction breakdown map + Dashboard to surface it, then render the chips in fixture_card/1 (extension point already in place).

title: xox knockout import + first-scorer matching
description: Extend FIFA import to knockout rounds (4-8): resolve firstSquadScored->side and firstPlayerScored->player name (fuzzy cross-source match vs openfootball). Blocked until knockout rounds open and a populated prediction/show sample exists. Group-stage import shipped under xox; see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-16-xox-fifa-import-design.md (Out of scope).

title: Admin by-fixture inline prediction editing
description: The admin by-fixture lens (/admin/predictions?view=fixture) is currently read-only (audit: shows each player's pick + flags missing). The spec (2026-06-15-admin-console-design.md) originally called for inline save here too. Defer-decision during a02: by-player entry already covers all data entry, so by-fixture save was scoped out. To build: turn each player row into an editable form (home/away goals, first_scorer_side + first_scorer_player, booster) saving via Predictions.admin_upsert_prediction/1 (already exists, currently only called from tests; it handles the transactional booster-clear). Watch the one-booster-per-round rule across the by-fixture view.

title: Cache/scope Standings.leaderboard/0 (recomputed per dashboard load)
description: Predictex.Dashboard.for_player/2 calls Standings.leaderboard/0 on every /predictions page load, which scores all players × all predictions × all completed fixtures just to extract one player's entry and rank — O(league size) per view, no caching. Fine for the current ~15-player league; revisit (ETS cache, per-request memo, or a scoped query) if the league grows or page load gets slow.

title: Flags: commit openfootball team-name snapshot + regression test
description: PredictexWeb.Flags is keyed on the live openfootball 2026 feed strings, verified once by fetch-and-diff during implementation. CI cannot hit the live feed, so coverage can silently rot as WC2026 placeholders (e.g. W73, 1A) resolve to real nations that then fall back to ⚽. Commit a snapshot of the feed's team-name list as a test fixture and assert the flag map covers every real nation in it. Cosmetic-only impact (unmapped → ⚽), hence low priority.
notes: Partially addressed by 7ux: lib/predictex/fifa/cohort.ex @aliases is a data-verified FIFA<->openfootball name map (8 divergences diffed from live squads.json vs worldcup.json). Remaining c9s work: commit an openfootball team-name snapshot + regression test.

title: xox: derive bookmarklet import URL from endpoint, not hardcoded @import_url
description: ImportLive hardcodes @import_url="https://wc-predict.davewil.dev/import". Correct for prod, but a dev/staging-rendered bookmarklet would point at the wrong host. Derive from PredictexWeb.Endpoint URL config so each env renders a correct bookmarklet. Minor (single-operator deploy today). Found in xox final review.
close_reason: Bookmarklet URL now derived from PredictexWeb.Endpoint.url() so the dev page emits a localhost bookmarklet and prod emits the prod one. Was blocking local manual validation (dev page emitted a prod-pointing bookmarklet -> 404 on undeployed prod).

title: Harden Predictions.save_round_row against direct-API misuse
description: Code-review (a02) flagged two robustness gaps in admin_save_round_predictions/3 that are NOT reachable from the by-player LiveView (which always passes atom-keyed, same-round rows) but would bite a future direct caller: (1) save_round_row stamps the caller-supplied round_id without verifying the fixture belongs to that round, so a cross-round fixture would persist with a wrong denormalized round_id — admin_upsert_prediction/1 and create_prediction/2 both re-derive round_id from the fetched fixture and are immune; (2) the nil/nil and booster_on_blank clauses match atom keys only, so a string-keyed row map falls through and row.fixture_id raises KeyError. Fix: re-derive round_id per row from the fixture, and/or normalise keys at the context boundary. Low priority — current sole caller is safe.
